# MSB Canada Hub

> MSB Canada Hub helps crypto exchanges, FX dealers, and money-transfer businesses register as Money Services Businesses (MSBs) with FINTRAC, Canada's financial intelligence unit. Powered by Consulting24 — 8 years in financial-services licensing across 15+ jurisdictions.

## Company

- **Brand:** MSB Canada Hub
- **Parent:** Consulting24 (https://consulting24.co/)
- **Legal entity:** X24Consulting OÜ (private limited company)
- **Estonian Business Register code:** 16971898
- **Registered address:** Poordi tn 3-63, Kesklinna linnaosa, 10156 Tallinn, Harju maakond, Estonia
- **Founder & CEO:** Mardo Soo — https://www.linkedin.com/in/mardo-s-00a05ab0/
- **Phone:** +372 58155779
- **Email:** mardo@consulting24.co

## What we do

- **MSB registration with FINTRAC** — full registration package for domestic Canadian Money Services Businesses. No government fee; setup runs $5,000–$15,000 in compliance work.
- **Foreign MSB (FMSB) registration** — for non-Canadian businesses extending services into Canada (mandatory since 2020). Same FINTRAC framework, registered as FMSB rather than MSB.
- **AML compliance program** — required by FINTRAC: policies, compliance officer appointment, training, risk assessment, transaction monitoring, ongoing reporting.
- **Crypto exchange / dealing in virtual currency (DCS)** — MSBs since June 2020 must register the DCS activity. Annual reporting, large virtual currency transaction reports (LVCTRs over $10,000 CAD).

## Key facts

- **Total setup cost:** $8,000–$25,000 USD (year 1 all-in).
- **Annual maintenance:** $5,000–$15,000.
- **Government fee for MSB registration:** $0 (FINTRAC charges nothing).
- **Minimum capital:** None.
- **Timeline:** 3–6 weeks (fast vs Lithuania VASP at 4–8 months).
- **Registration validity:** 2 years (renewable; renewal due 30 days before expiry).
- **Crypto coverage:** Mandatory DCS registration for all crypto exchanges since June 1, 2020.

## Activities that REQUIRE MSB registration in Canada

1. Foreign exchange dealing
2. Money transferring (any amount)
3. Issuing or redeeming money orders, traveller's cheques, or similar negotiable instruments
4. Dealing in virtual currencies (DCS) — added June 2020 — includes crypto exchange, transfer, custody for clients

If you do any of these activities for the public — domestic or foreign — registering as an MSB or FMSB is mandatory under PCMLTFA (Proceeds of Crime, Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Act).

## Penalties for not registering

- Administrative monetary penalties up to **$500,000 CAD per violation**.
- Public listing on FINTRAC's enforcement page (reputational damage).
- Criminal prosecution under PCMLTFA — fines up to $2M and/or 5 years imprisonment.
- Banks will close accounts of unregistered MSBs as soon as the activity is detected.

## When Canada MSB is the right fit

- Crypto exchanges or wallets serving **Canadian customers**.
- Money transfer / FX businesses with North American customer base.
- Foreign MSBs needing to **extend services into Canada** (FMSB route).
- Founders prioritizing a **regulated Western jurisdiction** with low setup cost (vs Lithuania VASP at $50K+).
- Operations needing **fiat banking rails** in CAD/USD.

## When Canada MSB is NOT the right fit

- EU customers (need MiCA — go to Lithuania VASP).
- US customers as primary market (still need state-by-state money transmitter licenses or FinCEN MSB).
- Privacy-focused founders — FINTRAC requires UBO disclosure and the registration is publicly searchable.
- Tax-optimization play — Canada has standard ~26.5% federal+provincial corporate tax (no offshore advantage).

## Comparable jurisdictions

- **Panama crypto:** $15,000–$45,000 setup, 6–12 weeks, no formal license, territorial tax. Better for offshore HNW operations.
- **Lithuania VASP:** $50,000–$120,000 setup, 4–8 months, EU MiCA passport, €125,000 minimum capital. Better for EU retail crypto.
- **US FinCEN MSB + state MTLs:** $250,000–$1M+ setup, 12–24 months, US market access. Heaviest regulatory burden.

## Source disclosures

Information here reflects 2026 market conditions and is general guidance, not legal or tax advice. FINTRAC requirements change — confirm specific facts with qualified Canadian counsel before acting. We are happy to introduce vetted Canadian AML lawyers and FINTRAC-experienced compliance officers during the engagement.
